commit.message:
feat(core): add skill_dependencies migration v10 (SMI-3134) (#286)

* feat(core): add skill_dependencies migration v10 (SMI-3134)

Add migration v10 creating the skill_dependencies table for the Skill
Dependency Intelligence Layer. Bumps SCHEMA_VERSION from 9 to 10.

The table stores declared and inferred dependency records with support
for 11 dep_type values (skill_hard, skill_soft, skill_peer, mcp_server,
model_minimum, model_capability, env_tool, env_os, env_node, cli_version,
conflict) and 3 dep_source values (declared, inferred_static,
inferred_coinstall). A unique index on (skill_id, dep_type, dep_target,
dep_source) prevents duplicate rows from repeated inference runs.

Co-Authored-By: claude-flow <ruv@ruv.net>

schema.ts exceeded the 500-line limit after Wave 1 added the v10

commit.message:
fix: trim Firebase credentials to remove trailing whitespace/newlines

The FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID and FIREBASE_CLIENT_EMAIL env vars on Vercel
were stored with trailing literal \n characters (set 6 days ago via
shell piping that appended newlines). This caused Firebase Admin to
initialize with 'pyxis-prod-e4ad9\n' as the project ID, making all
token verifications fail with 401.

Changes:
- Trim projectId and clientEmail before passing to cert()
- Keep the replace(/\n/g, '\n') for privateKey (literal backslash-n
  in the env var must be converted to actual newlines for PEM parsing)
- Add missing-credentials warning log for easier future debugging

Root cause: The two env vars on Vercel were also re-set with printf
(no trailing newline). FIREBASE_PRIVATE_KEY was re-set from .env.local
which correctly stores the key with literal \n sequences.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(marketplace): add google-workspace-cli plugin entry

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix TimeoutError catch: use Exception for Python <3.11 compat

concurrent.futures.TimeoutError != builtin TimeoutError before Python 3.11.
The except clause wasn't catching the timeout, causing rebuild to fail.

fix: lower name-part matching threshold to catch short first names

Was filtering out words < 4 chars, missing names like Jon, Ned, Rob,
Sam, Cat etc. Now skips only particles ≤ 2 chars. Also deduplicates
candidates via Set and splits alias words too.

returning stale/incomplete data and overriding the enriched R2 events,
causing old timestamps and missing transcripts.

commit.message:
fix: case-insensitive address lookup — checksum addresses returned empty tx data (#18)

* feat(etherscan): add tokenbalance, getminedblocks, getLogs, supply, price actions

Add five missing Etherscan-compatible API actions needed by third-party tools:
- module=account&action=tokenbalance: ERC-20 token balance for an address
- module=account&action=getminedblocks: blocks produced by a validator
- module=logs&action=getLogs: event logs with block range and topic filters
- module=stats&action=ethsupply: total QFC supply
- module=stats&action=ethprice: QFC price structure (zeroed, no oracle)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add /agents routes for AgentRegistry queries

Adds three endpoints that query the AgentRegistry contract at
0x7791dfa4d489f3d524708cbc0caa8689b76322b3 via RPC:
- GET /agents — list recent agent registrations (eth_getLogs)
- GET /agents/:agentId — get agent details (eth_call getAgent)
- GET /agents/owner/:address — list agents by owner (eth_call getAgentsByOwner)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: add missing token_prices migration (005) — caused /tokens 500 error

* fix: column name mismatches — rename transactions.data→input_data, add base_fee_per_gas, EIP-1559 fee columns

* fix(indexer): use input_data column + fix ON CONFLICT (hash) — transactions insert was crashing

* fix: replace t.data/tx.data with input_data in queries and transaction routes

* fix: address lookup case-insensitive — checksum addresses returned empty results

---------

commit.message:
feat: v4 — use CWP nginx for SSL, eliminate stunnel dependency

Previous versions required stunnel for SSL termination, which failed
on CentOS 7 due to old stunnel (4.56) lacking TLS 1.2 support.

v4 reconfigures CWP's own nginx instead:
- Public 2031/2087: nginx SSL proxy_pass to PHP 2FA gate on 127.0.0.1:2131
- Internal 2033/2034: CWP admin on localhost only
- Original config backed up for clean uninstall
- No external dependencies beyond PHP with curl and mail()

commit.message:
fix(config): enforce allow_edit_existing_files restriction in tool selection (#388) (#389)

Wire up the existing but unused `infer_task_edit_requirement()` heuristic
to the tool selection pipeline. When a prompt indicates editing intent
(fix, implement, refactor, etc.), tools with `allow_edit_existing_files =
false` are now filtered out during tier-based selection.

Three changes:
- `resolve_tool_and_model()` accepts `needs_edit` and forwards it to
  `resolve_tier_tool_rotated()` (was hardcoded `false`)
- New `resolve_tier_tool_filtered()` replaces the unfiltered fallback,
  `resolve_tier_tool()` now delegates to it with `needs_edit=false`
- `run_cmd_execute` calls `infer_task_edit_requirement()` and passes
  the result through the strategy resolution chain

Closes #388

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: move LINE verification check before tenantId resolution

The previous fix placed verification detection inside the !tenantId
block, but destination KV lookup could fail before reaching it.
Now parses body immediately after reading and returns 200 for empty
events array before any tenant logic runs.

commit.message:
fix: cell beads port config — root cause of recurring ce database loss

The cell rig's .beads/metadata.json was missing dolt_server_host and
dolt_server_port fields, and no dolt-server.port file existed. This caused
bd to connect to a wrong/ephemeral port (13649 instead of 3307), creating
the ce database on a phantom server. The database appeared created but
was not on the production Dolt server, causing all other agents to see
it as missing.

Fix: Added dolt-server.port file (3307) and server connection fields to
metadata.json. The ce database now exists on the production server.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Refactor daemon into parallel message + generation tasks with shared lock

The old daemon interleaved message processing and generation cycles in a
single sequential loop — messages sat in the queue until the next wake
interval, and a running cycle could block conversation responses for 30+
seconds.

New architecture (Option B):
- Message task: polls the Discord queue every 2s and processes messages
  immediately. Conversations feel near-instant.
- Generation task: runs gap→plan→generate→integrate on the configured
  interval, independently of message processing.
- Both tasks share an asyncio.Lock so they never write to the operation
  log, capability registry, or git working tree simultaneously. The
  message task holds the lock briefly (classify + respond). The
  generation task holds it for the full build sequence.

Also adds process_one() to discord_listener for single-message dequeue,
keeping process_pending() as a legacy wrapper.

117/117 tests passing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
perf: optimize ASSETS OVERVIEW + DATA tab, add SYNC review flow and CLIENT_PROVIDED protection

Phase 1: SYNC now pulls entries into Potential Matches as PENDING for manual
review instead of directly into Known Assets as CONFIRMED.

Phase 2: CLIENT_PROVIDED known assets are protected from accidental removal —
single removal requires confirmation dialog, bulk removal skips them. Visual
distinction with lock icon and cyan left-border accent.

Phase 3: ASSETS OVERVIEW cut from 10-15s to <2s — FP/validated enrichment
converted from O(N×M) to O(1) set lookups, domain matching pushed into SQL
(exact + LIKE ANY for subdomains), name/username matching pushed into SQL
with LIKE conditions. Added 60s TTL server-side cache with invalidation on
asset add/remove/confirm actions.

Phase 4: DATA tab backend caching — 30s TTL cache for targetFps,
targetValidated, knownAssets, and rowNotes lookups across paginated requests.

Phase 5: DATA tab progressive loading — "Load More" fetches next 500 rows
and appends to DOM (no full rebuild). "Load All" kept as fallback for
scroll-to-hash feature. Parallel /scaneventresultscount for accurate totals.

Phase 6: Potential Matches pagination — initial load of 200 matches with
"Load More" button. Server returns {items, total, pending_count} format.

commit.message:
Calm main graph physics; unify ego graph visuals; freeze after stabilization

Main network graph:
- gravitationalConstant: -60000 → -15000 (5x less repulsion, scales better)
- damping: 0.1 → 0.5 (5x more friction, stops bouncing)
- maxVelocity: 100 → 30 (nodes can't fly across the canvas)
- timestep: 0.5 → 0.35 (more stable simulation step)
- stabilization: 300 iters/25ms → 500 iters/100ms (fuller pre-settle)

Ego network graph:
- Replace precomputed NetworkX layout with stable_mode=True Barnes-Hut
- Same visual format as main graph (identical node/edge/color/label styling)
- Very gentle physics: gravity -8000, damping 0.8, maxVelocity 15
- 1000 stabilization iterations → fully settled before user sees it

Both graphs:
- JS injected before </body> listens for stabilizationIterationsDone and
  calls network.setOptions({physics:{enabled:false}}) to freeze the layout
  after the initial settle — no ongoing jitter, drag/zoom still work

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Interactive tutorial with animated demos and audio (#238)

## Summary

- Interactive tutorial overlay with punch-out highlights, step-by-step walkthrough of all spatch features
- Animated demos: shape creation, timbre spinning, color/formant cycling, patterns, blend modes, borders, duplicate/delete, undo/redo, randomize, harmonize, scene switching, ADSR corners, play button, share
- Opens in a dedicated `?tutorial` tab to isolate audio state from the user's existing spatch
- Tutorial audio uses `AudioEngine` directly instead of `PlaybackController`, eliminating async race conditions that caused audio to persist after dismissal

## Test plan

- [ ] Click tutorial button — opens new tab with `?tutorial`
- [ ] Step through all tutorial steps — audio plays/stops cleanly on each transition
- [ ] Close tutorial mid-step (X button or Escape) — audio stops immediately
- [ ] Advance quickly through audio steps — no lingering sound from previous steps
- [ ] Latched audio steps (step 0, color demo) sustain at envelope level until step change
- [ ] Tutorial tab closes on finish (after credits) or on close button
- [ ] Original tab's spatch is untouched after tutorial

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

EDGAR download intensity pipeline: aggregate index + firm-level panel extraction

Pipeline scripts for SEC EDGAR server log data (SRAF 2003-2015):
- config.R: paths, sample boundaries, source-quality exclusion windows,
  form-type regexes, bad-day detection parameters, extraction versioning
- 00_extract_edgar.R: filing-level extraction from yearly zips into
  monthly x CIK panel (10.2M rows, 502K CIKs) with form-type and
  filing-age breakdowns, month-level accession dedup for n_filings,
  daily cross-validation against Calendar_Summary.csv (exact match)
- 01_process_edgar.R: daily aggregate processing with two-pass bad-day
  detection, source-quality masking, monthly log-deviation + deseasonalize
  + quadratic detrend, quarterly aggregation
- 02_validate_edgar.R: countercyclicality validation via gap-aware CF
  band-pass filter and HAC recession regressions
- 03_plot_edgar.R: detrended attention series with recession shading

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: move PermanentSyncFailure to top-level, fix test assertions

- PermanentSyncFailure moved from companion object to top-level class
  in ApiService.kt — the nested-in-companion position caused an
  "Unresolved reference" error on CI despite being valid Kotlin syntax.
  Top-level is cleaner and unambiguous.
- SyncWorker references updated from ApiService.PermanentSyncFailure
  to just PermanentSyncFailure (same package, no import needed).
- SyncWorkerTest: fix expired-token test assertion — after the fallback
  full read SyncWorker re-acquires a fresh token and persists it, so
  the assertion is now assertEquals("bp-token", ...) not assertFalse.
- SyncWorkerTest: add missing recordHash to 3 SyncQueueEntity
  constructions broken by the Codex commit's schema change.
- Also push QR URL validation commit that was only committed locally.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: redesign dashboard to comparison-first layout with WhatIf cards

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: LALO（複勝◎1点・全額）プラン追加

3プラン体制に拡張:
  HAHO: 馬連◎軸2点 + 三連複ボックス1点（安定積み上げ）
  HALO: 三連複ボックス1点のみ（高配当特化）
  LALO: 複勝◎1点のみ（コツコツ複勝）← 新規

- predict_weekly.py: get_bets()にLALO追加、CSV出力列追加
- generate_results.py: LALO集計・後方互換マッピング追加
- app.py: 買い目ページにLALO選択肢、実績ページにLALOタブ追加
- simulate_patterns.py: 複勝専用パターン3種追加（比較分析用）

シミュ結果: 複勝◎1点 ROI 108-111% / 的中率66-68%（valid/test）

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: scaffold RFS Resume Builder MVP

React Router v7 + Tailwind v4 resume builder with:
- Landing page with drag-and-drop upload and "Start from Scratch"
- AI resume parser (GPT-4o primary, Claude fallback) for PDF/DOCX
- Two-panel editor with collapsible sections and live HTML preview
- PDF export via @react-pdf/renderer
- Recruiter best practices tips engine
- localStorage persistence (24hr expiry, no auth needed)
- Light theme with purple/indigo accents, DM Sans font
- Rate-limited API routes with magic bytes validation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
